problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 5000]

problems.title: Middle of the List
problems.prompt: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 100]
1 <= Node.val <= 100

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problems.prompt: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists into one sorted list by splicing together nodes from both lists. Return the head of the merged list.
constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in [0, 50]
Both lists are sorted in non-decreasing order

problems.title: Linked List Cycle
problems.prompt: Given head of a linked list, determine if the list has a cycle. A cycle means some node can be reached again by continuously following next pointers. Return true if there is a cycle, otherwise false.

examples.input: head = [3, 2, 0, -4], pos = 1 (tail connects to node index 1)

constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]
pos is -1 or a valid index

problems.title: Remove Nth From End
problems.prompt: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 30]
1 <= n <= size of list
0 <= Node.val <= 100

problems.title: Add Two Numbers
problems.prompt: You are given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers, stored in reverse order with one digit per node. Add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list, also in reverse order.
constraints: The number of nodes in each list is in [1, 100]
0 <= Node.val <= 9
It is guaranteed that the list represents a number that does not have leading zeros, except the number 0 itself

problems.title: Reverse Nodes in K-Group
problems.prompt: Given the head of a linked list, reverse the nodes of the list k at a time and return the modified list. k is a positive integer and is less than or equal to the length of the list. If the number of nodes remaining is not a multiple of k, leave those nodes as-is.
constraints: The number of nodes is in [1, 5000]
0 <= Node.val <= 1000
1 <= k <= number of nodes

problems.title: Copy List with Random
problems.prompt: A linked list of length n where each node contains an additional random pointer that can point to any node in the list or null. Construct a deep copy of the list. Each new node should have the same value and corresponding next/random structure, and none of the pointers in the new list should point to nodes in the original list.
constraints: Node.random is null or points to some node in the list
